Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 137. Clearing away Rubble from a Collapsed Wall (ABL 0329)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-na LUGAL be-lí-ia

(1) To the king, my lord: your servant Amar-ili.

o 22

ARAD-ka ma-mar—DINGIR

o 33

BÀD ša É.GAL

(3) We cleared away the wall of the palace which had fallen in and were starting to dig the foundations when the granary between the storehouse of the Palace Superintendent and the city wall fell down. The barley [......

o 44

ša im-qu-ta-a-ni

o 55

nu-up-ta-si-ik

o 66

ina UGU uš-še

o 77

ḫa-ra-aṣ-ṣi

o 88

ni-iq-ṭí-rib

o 99

É—kar-me ša bi-ir-ti

o 1010

É—ŠU.2 LÚv.šá—IGI—É.GAL

o 1111

ša bi-ir-ti BÀD

o 1212

it-tuq*-⸢ta?⸣

o 1313

ŠE.PAD-⸢MEŠ⸣ [x x x]

rest (about 3 lines) broken away

(Break)

Reverse
rr beginning (about 3 lines) broken away

r 1'1'

ina É—⸢ŠU*⸣.[2 x x]

(r 1) We have [...]ed and dumped it in the sto[rehouse of ...] and in the storehouse [of ...].

r 2'2'

ina É—ŠU.[2 x x bat-qu]

r 3'3'

ni-iṣ-ṣa-[bat x x x]

r 4'4'

ni-ta-⸢ba*-ak*⸣

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334212/.
